Bonasera begs Don Corleone for justice after the courts fail him, and Corleone accepts the plea but demands Bonasera swear loyalty in return as payment.
Michael Corleone lies to his entire family about his peaceful intentions, then in one coordinated move orders the simultaneous assassination of rival gang boss Sollozzo and a corrupt police captain.

Don Corleone leads a powerful crime family whose loyal enforcer Luca Brasi is sent to infiltrate a rival operation and is killed, triggering a war that forces the Don's youngest son into the family business.

Eve Harrington manipulates seasoned Broadway star Margo Channing by fabricating a tragic backstory to gain her sympathy and access to her world, then systematically lies to every person who helps her.
Eve lies to Karen Richards to secure a crucial role, then betrays Bill Sampson who championed her career, revealing herself as a calculating opportunist who has manipulated everyone around her.
Theater critic Addison deWitt discovers Eve's fraudulent past and confronts her with the truth, then manipulates Eve herself — turning her from predator into prey.

New Orleans district attorney Jim Garrison confronts the FBI, Secret Service, and Dallas Police as he seeks the truth behind Kennedy's assassination, compiling evidence that multiple conspirators were involved.
Jim Garrison discovers the Zapruder film and realizes the official account is impossible, then confronts the federal government itself as his investigation exposes layers of CIA deception.
Lee Harvey Oswald lies to the CIA and to Marina who eventually betrays him, while Jack Ruby kills Oswald before he can speak — silencing the only man who could reveal who ordered the assassination.
Jack Kelly leads the New York newsboys in a strike against publisher Pulitzer, allies with Spot Conlon's Brooklyn crew, and confronts Warden Snyder who hunts him as a runaway.
Jack Kelly briefly betrays the Newsies by making a secret deal with Pulitzer to escape to Santa Fe, but the other boys discover his betrayal and he must decide where his loyalty truly lies.
A gang of underpaid young newspaper sellers confront a powerful media baron who raised their distribution fees, organizing across rival boroughs and allying with a reporter who reveals their cause.
Adonis Johnson confronts Duke the trainer who refuses to teach him, then seeks Rocky Balboa in Philadelphia — the only man willing to train the illegitimate son of his greatest rival Apollo Creed.

Mary Anne Creed protects Adonis from knowing his true origins while Rocky Balboa teaches him to fight, pushing him past his limits even as Rocky himself is secretly battling a life-threatening illness.

FBI agent Carl Hanratty relentlessly seeks and confronts Frank Abagnale Jr across multiple states and countries, while Frank successfully lies to banks, airlines, and hospitals by impersonating a pilot, doctor, and lawyer.

Frank Abagnale Jr learns how to impersonate authority figures so convincingly that he manipulates entire institutions, but the one person he cannot fully deceive is the FBI agent who has spent years chasing him.

Lieutenant John Dunbar confronts a hostile superior officer before being posted alone to an abandoned frontier fort, where he gradually builds trust with a Lakota tribe by learning their language and customs.
John Dunbar confronts Kicking Bird in their tense first encounter, then slowly allies with the Lakota through shared hunting and mutual respect, ultimately realizing the tribe is his true family.

A Union soldier who has lost faith in the war finds himself alone among the Sioux, learns their ways from a woman who speaks English, and is transformed from an outsider into a member of the community.
Terry Prescott loves his sister Sammy but keeps abandoning her — returning broke after years away and forcing her to confront whether she can forgive him while juggling her own troubled life.
Amy confronts Terry Prescott about his destructive behavior toward his sister, while Rudy Kolinski also confronts Sammy about letting her brother disrupt her life and her son's stability.
Hancock saves Aaron's life then teaches and confronts him about his attitude, while Mary protects both Hancock and Aaron from criminals seeking revenge — including the villain Clyde.

A reckless superhero lies to a young man about his past, manipulates the people around him, and must confront Mary who reveals the truth about their ancient shared history and why they cannot stay together.
Mateo betrays and lies to both Judit Garcia and Harry Caine about his true identity, while Ernesto Senior kills Lena — the woman Harry loves and had been desperately trying to protect.

A blind filmmaker hides his real identity while Judit sacrifices her own happiness to protect Diego, and the truth about a tragic love affair between a director and his actress is revealed through rediscovered footage.
Harry betrays obsessive producer Ernesto Martel who controls Lena, then saves Lena and loses his sight in the accident that kills her — spending years lying about who he is to escape Martel's reach.
